reasoning:
As of April 12, 2026, there are only 18 days remaining until the deadline of May 1, 2026. Recent statements from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on April 7-9, 2026, indicate that he remains open to a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin, but continues to insist on neutral territory, while explicitly rejecting Moscow or Kyiv. Furthermore, there are no scheduled dates or agreed-upon venues, and Putin has historically dismissed the idea of a direct meeting. 

Given the logistical and diplomatic complexities of organizing an in-person summit between wartime leaders, it is exceedingly unlikely that one could be arranged from scratch and executed within an 18-day window without any groundwork having been finalized. A related prediction market assessing the likelihood of a meeting occurring by June 30, 2026, traded at around 4% only two weeks prior, confirming that the baseline market sentiment for a near-term meeting is exceptionally low. Thus, the probability of them meeting before May 1, 2026, is functionally at the floor of acceptable probabilities (2%).
